facts: Mercury is the smallest rocky planet, only ~4,879 km (3,032 miles) wide.
But beneath its thin crust lies an ENORMOUS metallic core, around 3,600 km (2,200 miles) across.
This means Mercury's core makes up an astonishing ~85% of the planet's total diameter!
idea.payoff: It's less of a planet and more like a colossal iron heart with a thin rocky shell!
idea.title: Mercury: Mostly CORE! 🤯🪐

scenes.visual: Full view of Mercury, slowly rotating against a starry background. A subtle, mysterious glow emanates from its center.

scenes.text: Smallest rocky planet: ~4,879 km (3,032 miles) wide.
scenes.visual: Mercury (full view) with a measurement line showing its diameter. Faint outline of Earth for size comparison.

scenes.text: But its core is ~3,600 km (2,200 miles) across!
scenes.visual: Mercury transitions to a cutaway cross-section. The large, metallic core is brightly highlighted in gold/orange, contrasting with the thin grey-brown crust and mantle.
The Mercury cross-section remains. The core dramatically expands slightly and pulses with a strong glow, with a large, bold '85%' graphic overlaid and integrated visually, emphasizing its proportion.

scenes.text: Less planet, more colossal iron heart with a thin rocky shell!
scenes.visual: A simplified, highly dramatic cross-section of Mercury. The core dominates the screen, glowing intensely like a massive heart, with only a very thin, almost fragile-looking rocky outer shell visible. The core pulsates gently.
